coreQuestion: Why does Claude Code lose memory every session?
viewerSituation: Developer using Claude Code daily, frustrated by repeated context loss
openingTension: You paid for Claude Code, why does it act like its first day?
personalCase: Building a Remotion video system with persistent CLAUDE.md
boundaries: Not covering API-level memory, only CLI tool
finalTakeaway: CLAUDE.md is your project persistent brain
immediateAction: Create a CLAUDE.md in your project root
draftBody: You open Claude Code in your project for the hundredth time. You explain your folder structure again. You explain your conventions again. You explain your decisions again. It is like a new employee every single day. But there is a file that fixes this. CLAUDE.md. A single markdown file in your project root that Claude Code reads on every session start. I tested this for 30 days across 3 projects. The results were clear: session setup time dropped from 5 minutes to 30 seconds.

viewerStateChange.viewerStateBefore: Claude Code is unreliable, loses context every time, nothing can fix it
viewerStateChange.viewerStateAfter: CLAUDE.md solves this with a specific mechanism, it is a file with clear steps and real boundaries
viewerStateChange.stateChangeReason: Showing the actual file, its growth over 30 days, and the before/after metrics

contentDesignRationale.whyThisOpening: Direct pain point recognition in 2 seconds, immediate self-identification
contentDesignRationale.whyThisCase: First-hand 30-day experiment across 3 projects, real metrics
contentDesignRationale.whyThisEvidenceOrder: Problem first, then mechanism, then proof, then boundaries
contentDesignRationale.whyThisRevealOrder: Start with frustration, reveal the file, show growth, state limits
contentDesignRationale.whyThisBoundary: Only cover CLI-level memory, not API memory management

mustBeSeen: Developer explaining folder structure for the 100th time
canBeSpoken: Background on why this happens technically
needsVisualProcess: State change from forgetting to remembering
visualHandoff.whyThisNeedsVisualSupport: Abstract concept of memory loss needs concrete visual metaphor of repeated setup
Evidence must be visible to be believed, not just described
